response.justification: Sarah Smith is fundamentally a web and full-stack engineer whose primary expertise lies in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no meaningful C++ experience beyond approximately six months at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a bachelor thesis project. The role mandates 5-10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows, strong SQL Server or Oracle experience, and demonstrated capital markets or fixed-income domain knowledge — none of which this candidate possesses. Her closest financial-services exposure is a Node.js/TypeScript role at RIDE Capital focused on German investment-account tooling, which does not constitute capital markets or fixed-income domain expertise.
strengths: CI/CD pipeline experience (GitLab CI, Docker, Artifactory) aligns with a desirable optional skill
Java development background at Altom Consulting satisfies one optional desirable skill
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, pairing) indicate code quality discipline
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI in 2014-2015, one thesis project) versus the 5-10 years mandatory requirement
Zero capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking domain knowledge
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle experience, nor Windows platform development — both mandatory requirements
